title: COSI Transient Alert
description: Single-stream COSI transient alerts. COSI triggers onboard via its BGO shields; roughly half of gamma-ray bursts additionally produce significant germanium-detector (Compton-mode) signal, enabling fine localization, spectroscopy, and polarization. This schema also accepts transients identified only in ground analysis (with no onboard trigger), reported with alert_tense 'archival'. The transient class is reported via the required classification field so that one stream serves GRBs and other gamma-ray transients. Recommended classification keys (mutually exclusive, probabilities sum to 1): 'GRB', 'SGR', 'Solar', 'TGF', 'Local Particles', 'Uncertain', 'Noise'. A retraction reports its revised classification, typically dominated by 'Noise' or 'Uncertain'. Localization: healpix_url is the authoritative localization for every notice; ra/dec give the maximum-a-posteriori point of that map; ra_dec_error should be populated only when a circle (scalar) or ellipse (array) is a faithful summary of the contour, which is frequently NOT the case for Compton-derived regions (annulus/arc shapes) - consumers should use the HEALPix map for follow-up planning.

ged_snr.description: Signal-to-noise of the germanium-detector (Compton-mode) signal. Present only when significant GeD signal exists. Its presence is the indicator that Compton-derived products (fine localization, Spectral, polarization assessment) are available or forthcoming; its absence means COSI has only shield-derived information for this event.

triggered_shields.description: Trigger status of the 6 effective BGO shields; true if the shield contributed to the onboard trigger. Absent for transients identified only in ground analysis (no onboard trigger).

prob_short.description: Preliminary probability [dimensionless, 0-1] that the burst is a short GRB, defined by the conventional T90 < 2 s boundary. This is a preliminary estimate intended for rapid kilonova-style follow-up triage and is superseded once a measured t90 is reported in a later notice.

polarization_candidate.description: True if COSI believes the burst is bright enough for a useful polarization measurement; false if it is not expected to support a useful polarization measurement.
